name: Day-Met
description_short: Treadwell Credit Union (formerly DayMet) is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union serving the Miami Valley with roots dating to 1957, offering checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services.
description_long:
Treadwell Credit Union began in 1957 as a credit union built by and for General Motors employees in the Miami Valley region. The organization recently rebranded from DayMet Credit Union to Treadwell Credit Union, honoring its legacy while signaling forward momentum. As a federally-insured credit union (NCUA-insured), it operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit institution dedicated to enriching members' financial well-being through community partnership.

Treadwell offers comprehensive financial services including savings accounts, checking accounts with rewards (Trail Blazer and Gem City accounts), certificates, personal loans, mortgages, and online banking. Members gain access to 5,000+ surcharge-free ATMs nationwide through shared branching networks. Digital services include It'sMe24/7 Online Banking, a mobile app, mobile check deposit, bill pay, and fund transfers. The credit union also provides community recognition programs like the Do Good for Dayton Award and Golden Apple Award for teachers and coaches.

Treadwell distinguishes itself through strong community ties and member benefits integrated with local partnerships. Their checking accounts include BaZing app discounts at local Miami Valley businesses, early direct deposit, cell phone protection, roadside assistance, and identity theft protection. The credit union maintains 5 physical branches in the Dayton area and operates a telephone teller service (CU Talk). Their recent rebranding emphasizes stability—operating for nearly 70 years—while modernizing services for current members.

As a credit union, Treadwell is limited to serving members who meet eligibility requirements (typically GM employees, family members, or community membership in the Miami Valley). While they offer competitive rates and comprehensive services, members must qualify for membership and may face fewer branch locations than large national banks. The organization's focus on community and member service is genuine, though service scope is regional rather than national.
services: Checking accounts (Trail Blazer and Gem City accounts with BaZing benefits)
pros: Access to 5,000+ surcharge-free ATM locations nationwide through shared branching network
BaZing app provides discounts at local Miami Valley businesses with checking accounts
Checking accounts include early direct deposit, cell phone protection, roadside assistance, and identity theft protection
Mobile deposit available through It'sMe24/7 Mobile App for check deposits via smartphone
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means profits benefit members rather than shareholders
Nearly 70-year operating history with roots in GM employee community
Active community engagement programs including Do Good for Dayton Award and Golden Apple Award nominations
24/7 online and mobile banking access with bill pay and fund transfer capabilities
cons: Membership eligibility limited to GM employees, family members, and eligible community members in Miami Valley area
Only 5 physical branch locations in Dayton region compared to national banks with hundreds of branches
Mobile deposit requires 90-day account opening period before eligibility
Regional focus means limited convenience for members outside Miami Valley area
Upcoming system upgrade (Feb 27-28, 2026) will briefly affect service availability
best_for: GM employees and their families seeking member-owned financial institution with community roots
Miami Valley residents wanting local credit union with competitive rates and community involvement
Members prioritizing access to nationwide ATM network and digital-first banking
Consumers valuing identity theft protection, roadside assistance, and local business discounts built into checking accounts
diagnosis: Treadwell Credit Union is best for GM employees, family members, and Miami Valley community residents seeking a stable, member-owned financial institution with competitive rates and strong community engagement. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted (primarily GM-connected), and the institution serves a regional rather than national market, making it unsuitable for consumers outside the Miami Valley requiring extensive local branch access.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Day-Met — Credit Union in Dayton, OH | CreditDoc
meta_description: Day-Met — NCUA-insured credit union in Dayton, OH. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
